title: Replace hardcoded requeue intervals with constants
summary: All 9 hardcoded requeue intervals in production code (4 in reconcile_secrets.go, 3 in reconcile_database.go, 1 in reconcile_bootstrap.go, 1 in reconcile_deployment.go) replaced with named constants defined in requeue_intervals.go. All 18 test assertions updated to reference the same constants. The time import removed from all 8 files where it is no longer needed. Integration test eventuallyLongTimeout now derives from 2*RequeueDatabaseWait. Constants file has SPDX header, CC-0044 feature ID, and descriptive doc comments for each constant. No behavioral change — all default values are identical to the original hardcoded values.

tests_checklist.item: All existing unit tests pass (42 tests, 0.666s)
Test assertions updated from literals to named constants (18 replacements)
Integration test timeout derives from RequeueDatabaseWait
No behavioral change in test expectations

code_quality_checklist.item: Follows existing code patterns (SPDX header, package-level constants, doc comments)
Single Responsibility: constants isolated in dedicated file
No magic numbers remain in production or test code
No dead code or unused imports

security_checklist.item: No security-relevant changes (pure refactoring of time constants)

architecture_checklist.item: Solution is as simple as possible (package-level constants, no over-engineering)
No scope creep (no CR-configurable overrides added, staying within requested scope)
Constants are package-scoped (not exported beyond controller package)

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No duplicated code — constants defined once, used everywhere
No unnecessary abstractions (plain const block, not a config struct)

fail_fast_checklist.item: Not applicable — no error paths changed

defensive_checklist.item: Not applicable — no external inputs changed

verification.golangci_lint: PASS — 1 pre-existing issue in unchanged file (reconcile_networkpolicy_test.go:540 QF1008), not related to this change
